summary.business_type: Free web-based SaaS tool (image compression, browser-only / privacy-first)
summary.stack: Cloudflare (CDN/WAF), React SPA + Vite, static prerender per locale, GA4

top_findings.title: Canonical uses non-www but server serves www
top_findings.impact: Both www.compresspic.top and compresspic.top return 200 with self-referencing canonicals — Google has to pick one and may flip-flop. Both get indexed as duplicate.

top_findings.title: Trailing-slash 307 redirect mismatches canonical/sitemap
top_findings.impact: Canonical/sitemap declare /en-US/ but server 307s to /en-US (no slash). Crawl budget waste and canonical-target inaccessibility.

top_findings.title: All unknown URLs return 200 (SPA shell fallback, no 404)
top_findings.impact: Tested /about, /privacy, /blog, /random all return 200 with the homepage HTML. Search engines may index phantom pages. 404.html exists locally but isn't served.

top_findings.title: No llms.txt (route returns SPA shell)
top_findings.impact: /llms.txt returns the SPA HTML, not a real llms.txt. LLM crawlers (Anthropic, OpenAI, Cursor) get garbage when they follow the llmstxt.org convention.

top_findings.title: SoftwareApplication schema missing author, datePublished, softwareVersion
top_findings.impact: Google cannot link this app to its author identity. No version metadata. Limits Knowledge Graph eligibility.

quick_wins: Add <link rel='preload' as='image' href='/assets/frog-hero-bg-1600.webp'> to <head> — saves 200–500ms LCP
Long-cache /assets/* with Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000, immutable
Expand zh-CN/zh-TW/ja-JP meta descriptions from 45/43/74 chars to 90–120 chars
Create a real /llms.txt with a plain-text site description
Add Organization schema linking to github.com/joye61/pic-smaller

categories.name: Technical SEO
what_works: HTTPS enforced via Cloudflare
robots.txt properly configured with sitemap reference
Sitemap exists with all 9 locales
Cloudflare CDN with HTTP/2 + alt-svc (HTTP/3) enabled
AI-training opt-out via Content-Signal header (search=yes,ai-train=no)
WASM assets blocked from crawling (Disallow: /assets/*.wasm$)

findings.title: Canonical/www mismatch (no-www canonical but www server)
findings.description: Every page's <link rel='canonical'> and hreflang alternates use https://compresspic.top/... (no www). The server actually responds at https://www.compresspic.top/... and 200s the no-www version too (no redirect). Each version has a self-referencing canonical, so both are indexable as duplicates.
findings.recommendation: Choose one host. Recommended: keep www as canonical (it's where users land), set Cloudflare redirect rule compresspic.top/* -> 301 -> https://www.compresspic.top/:splat. Update canonical, hreflang, sitemap <loc>, OG/Twitter URLs to www.compresspic.top.

findings.title: Trailing-slash 307 redirect mismatches canonical/sitemap
findings.description: https://www.compresspic.top/en-US/ returns 307 -> https://www.compresspic.top/en-US (stripping slash). Sitemap and canonical declare URLs WITH trailing slash (https://compresspic.top/en-US/). Google fetches the canonical URL, gets a 307 redirect before landing on the actual content.
findings.recommendation: Pick a canonical slash form. Either: (a) remove the 307 redirect, redirect the reverse (/en-US -> /en-US/) and keep canonical with slash, or (b) keep the no-slash form and update canonical + sitemap to /en-US.

findings.title: All unknown paths return 200 with SPA shell (no 404)
findings.description: /about, /privacy, /terms, /contact, /blog, /help, /random-nonexistent-page all return HTTP 200 with the index.html shell (5377 bytes). The dist/404.html file exists locally but is not served.
findings.recommendation: Configure Cloudflare Pages or Worker: any path not matching the locale pattern (^/$ or ^/(en-US|zh-TW|ja-JP|ko-KR|fr-FR|es-ES|tr-TR|fa-IR)$) should serve 404.html with HTTP 404 status. This prevents phantom pages from being indexed.

findings.title: No security headers (CSP, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy)
findings.description: Response headers include only content-type, cache-control, cf-*, nel, report-to, server, alt-svc. No CSP, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy.
findings.recommendation: Add via Cloudflare Transform Rules: X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin, X-Frame-Options: DENY, Permissions-Policy: camera=(), microphone=(), geolocation=(). For CSP, a strict policy is hard with GA + WASM + Antd CSS-in-JS — start with report-only mode.

findings.title: Static asset cache-control is max-age=0
findings.description: Every response, including content-hashed assets (index-CyHHZpPs.js, frog-hero-bg-1600.webp), returns cache-control: public, max-age=0, must-revalidate. Returning users revalidate every request.
findings.recommendation: Split caching: /assets/* -> public, max-age=31536000, immutable (filenames are hashed). HTML/robots.txt/sitemap.xml -> public, max-age=300 (so updates propagate fast).

categories.name: Content Quality
what_works: Clear, factual product description across all 9 locales
Privacy-first positioning is consistent in title, description, JSON-LD, and FAQ
Pre-render pipeline per locale (scripts/prerender-locales.mjs) — each locale gets a localized <head>
JSON-LD FAQPage with 3 concrete Q&A pairs

findings.title: Static body is empty — no visible H1/H2 in pre-rendered HTML
findings.description:
Each locale's pre-rendered HTML has only
in the body. The hero text, features, and FAQ body are all rendered client-side. Crawlers that don't execute JS see only the meta description. Affects Bing, Baidu, in-app browsers, and AI crawlers.
findings.recommendation: Extend scripts/prerender-locales.mjs to also inline visible body content: <h1> (localized hero title), <h2> (feature sections), FAQ Q&A as plain HTML. The body can still be replaced by React on hydration (use dangerouslySetInnerHTML or a parallel non-hydrated block).

findings.title: Short meta descriptions in CJK locales (zh-CN 45ch, zh-TW 43ch, ja-JP 74ch)
findings.description: Meta description is the primary content snippet shown to users without JS rendering. zh-CN at 45 Chinese characters is well below the 90–155 char sweet spot Google typically displays.
findings.recommendation: Expand CJK descriptions to 80–120 chars. Include: supported formats (PNG/JPEG/WebP/AVIF/GIF/SVG), batch, free, browser support. Example zh-CN: '青蛙压缩是一款免费在线图片压缩工具，支持 PNG、JPEG、WebP、AVIF、GIF、SVG 等格式。所有处理都在浏览器本地完成，图片不上传服务器，保护隐私，可批量压缩，日常发送、上传网页都能用。'

findings.title: No About / Privacy Policy / Terms / Contact pages
findings.description: No author identity, no privacy policy, no terms of service, no contact email visible on the homepage. Limits E-E-A-T signals and trust.
findings.recommendation: Add /about (or /docs) page with author, project history, GitHub link (joye61/pic-smaller), MIT license. Add a minimal /privacy policy page stating the no-collection architecture. Both can be pre-rendered and added to sitemap.

findings.title: Single indexable page per locale (8 + 1 root)
findings.description: With only one page per locale, long-tail keyword coverage depends on the homepage ranking for everything. Limits topical authority.
findings.recommendation: Add 3-5 use-case landing pages: /compress-for-email, /compress-for-web, /compress-for-whatsapp, /png-vs-webp. Each pre-rendered with localized meta + FAQ. Adds 4-5x indexable URLs per locale.

categories.name: On-Page SEO
what_works: Title pattern 'Brand - Descriptor' consistent across all 9 locales
All hreflang alternates declared correctly (9 locales + x-default)
Comprehensive OG/Twitter tags including image dimensions, type, alt
Self-referencing canonical on every page (modulo www mismatch)
Keyword targeting aligned with locale (图片压缩, image compression, 画像圧縮, etc.)

findings.title: x-default is zh-CN (not English)
findings.description: hreflang x-default is set to https://compresspic.top/ (the Chinese homepage). English-speaking searchers landing on / will see Chinese content. If you want English-first global discoverability, set x-default to /en-US/.
findings.recommendation: If the site is primarily a Chinese tool, keep current x-default. If targeting global English-speaking users, change x-default to /en-US/.

findings.title: No internal links in static HTML (no language picker)
findings.description: All navigation is rendered client-side. Users without JS (and crawlers without JS execution) have no way to reach other locales from the homepage. No internal link equity flows.
findings.recommendation: Add a simple language picker to the prerendered body (footer or top-right corner), listing all 9 locales as English etc.

categories.name: Schema / Structured Data
what_works: Valid JSON-LD across all 9 locales
Appropriate type selection: WebSite + SoftwareApplication + FAQPage
Localized strings (name, description, FAQ Q&A) per locale
Offer with price=0, priceCurrency=USD correctly identifies free app

findings.title: SoftwareApplication missing author, datePublished, softwareVersion
findings.description: SoftwareApplication schema has only @id, @type, applicationCategory, operatingSystem, url, description, image, offers. Missing author (linking to joye61/pic-smaller), datePublished, softwareVersion (currently 1.1.0), featureList.
findings.recommendation: Add fields: author: { @type: Organization, name: 'joye61', url: 'https://github.com/joye61/pic-smaller' }, datePublished: '2024-01-01', softwareVersion: '1.1.0', featureList: ['Local compression', 'No server upload', 'Multiple formats', 'Batch support', 'Open source MIT']

findings.title: No Organization schema separate from SoftwareApplication
findings.description: Google Knowledge Graph needs an Organization entity to link the site to a brand identity. Currently no standalone Organization schema.
findings.recommendation: Add a separate Organization block (not in @graph): { @type: Organization, name: 'joye61', url: 'https://github.com/joye61/pic-smaller', logo: 'https://compresspic.top/logo.svg' }

findings.title: FAQPage only has 3 Q&A (rich result policy restricted)
findings.description: Google restricted FAQ rich results to government/health sites in 2023. For other sites, FAQ schema still helps AI extraction but won't show as rich snippets. Currently 3 Q&A which is minimal.
findings.recommendation: Expand FAQ to 6-10 Q&A: supported formats, max file size, browser compatibility, mobile support, comparison vs other tools, troubleshooting. Even without SERP rich results, AI crawlers extract these heavily.

categories.name: Performance (CWV)
what_works: Gzip compression enabled (67-70% ratio) on all text
Hero image already in WebP format
Static asset bundle hashed for cache-busting
Cloudflare CDN with HTTP/2 + HTTP/3 (alt-svc) enabled
HTML payload small (5-7 KB pre-rendered)

findings.title: No preload hints for hero image or main JS bundle
findings.description: Only preconnect to googletagmanager.com is declared. No <link rel='preload' as='image' href='/assets/frog-hero-bg-1600.webp'> and no modulepreload for /assets/index-CyHHZpPs.js. Hero LCP waits for HTML -> JS parse -> image fetch sequence.
findings.recommendation: Add to <head>: <link rel='preload' as='image' href='/assets/frog-hero-bg-1600.webp' type='image/webp' imagesrcset='...' imagesizes='...'> and <link rel='modulepreload' href='/assets/index-CyHHZpPs.js'>. Expected LCP improvement: 200-500ms on mobile.

findings.title: Antd bundled in full (581 KB JS / 188 KB gzipped)
findings.description: Main JS chunk is large because Antd 5.x ships CSS-in-JS and barrel imports. Most pages use only a handful of Antd components.
findings.recommendation: Switch from `import { Button, Slider } from 'antd'` to direct imports `import Button from 'antd/es/button'`. Add @ant-design/icons selective imports. Expected savings: 100-200 KB gzipped.

findings.title: Hero WebP not responsive (1600x686 served to mobile)
findings.description: Same 109 KB hero image is served to all viewport sizes. Mobile users pay for desktop-sized assets.
findings.recommendation: Generate 800w and 1200w variants. Use <picture><source media='(min-width: 768px)' srcset='/assets/frog-hero-bg-1200.webp'><source srcset='/assets/frog-hero-bg-800.webp'></picture>. Expected savings: 30-50% on mobile.

categories.name: AI Search Readiness
what_works: Clear factual product description suitable for AI extraction
FAQPage JSON-LD provides Q&A for AI summarization
Cloudflare Content-Signal header (search=yes, ai-train=no) — opt-out of training while allowing retrieval
Multi-language coverage declared via hreflang

findings.title: llms.txt returns the SPA shell HTML, not a real llms.txt
findings.description: /llms.txt returns HTTP 200 but the body is the homepage HTML (5377 bytes), not a real llms.txt. LLM crawlers following the llmstxt.org convention (Anthropic, OpenAI, Cursor, Windsurf) extract garbage. This is worse than not having one.
findings.recommendation: Create a real /llms.txt (plain text or markdown) describing the site for LLMs. Suggested content: product name, what it does, when to recommend, when not to, languages, technology stack, GitHub link. Suggested llms.txt content provided in findings/geo.md.

findings.title: All major AI training crawlers blocked
findings.description: GPTBot, ClaudeBot, Google-Extended, Bytespider, CCBot, Applebot-Extended, meta-externalagent, Amazonbot are all Disallow: /. This is a privacy-first choice but eliminates AI citation opportunities.
findings.recommendation: Keep `ai-train=no` for training purposes. Consider allowing specific retrieval-only bots: OAI-SearchBot, PerplexityBot, Claude-User, Claude-SearchBot, Applebot. These enable AI assistants to cite your site without contributing to training data.

findings.title: No Organization / author identity for AI to attribute
findings.description: AI assistants prefer to cite named entities. The site has no Organization schema, no GitHub link from the homepage, no About page. AI may hesitate to recommend an 'anonymous' tool.
findings.recommendation: Add Organization schema linking to github.com/joye61/pic-smaller. Add a small footer link to the GitHub repo. Consider adding an /about page.

findings.title: x-default lang mismatch (zh-CN as default but brand is multilingual)
findings.description: For users outside any declared locale, x-default routes to Chinese. AI assistants with locale detection may not surface this site to English-speaking users as confidently.
findings.recommendation: Consider setting x-default to the most universal landing page (en-US) so non-zh-CN/non-declared locales land in English.

categories.name: Images
what_works: WebP format for hero image (saves 25-35% vs JPEG/PNG)
OG image includes width, height, type, alt attributes
SVG favicon (vector, modern)
Image dimensions (1600x686) appropriate for OG

findings.title: Hero image has no responsive variants
findings.description: Single 1600x686 hero WebP (109 KB) served to all viewports. Mobile users download desktop-sized assets.
findings.recommendation: Generate 800w and 1200w variants, use <picture> or srcset. Save 30-50% on mobile.

findings.title: No apple-touch-icon declared
findings.description: iOS Safari will use the SVG favicon at low quality when adding to home screen.
findings.recommendation: Add apple-touch-icon (180x180 PNG or SVG if targeting iOS 18+).

items.title: Resolve canonical/www mismatch
steps: Decide canonical host: recommend www.compresspic.top
Add Cloudflare redirect rule: compresspic.top/* -> 301 -> https://www.compresspic.top/:splat
Update canonical, hreflang, sitemap <loc>, OG/Twitter URLs to use www.compresspic.top
Verify with curl -I https://compresspic.top/ returns 301 to www

items.title: Resolve trailing-slash 307 redirect
steps: Decide: keep slash or remove slash
If removing: drop the 307 redirect, update canonical + sitemap to no-slash URLs
If keeping: redirect /en-US -> /en-US/ (reverse direction)
Re-submit sitemap in GSC

items.title: Configure 404 handling for non-existent paths
steps: Add Cloudflare Pages _redirects: /:splat  /404.html  404  (excluding locale paths)
Verify: curl -I https://www.compresspic.top/about returns 404 with 404.html body
Verify locale paths still 200: curl -I https://www.compresspic.top/en-US returns 200

items.title: Create real /llms.txt
steps: Write plain-text site description: product name, value props, use cases, tech stack, GitHub link
Add to scripts/prerender-locales.mjs so each locale gets its own /llms-{locale}.txt
Add route in Cloudflare Pages: /llms.txt -> /llms-en-US.txt (or per-locale)
Validate: curl https://www.compresspic.top/llms.txt returns plain text, not HTML

items.title: Long-cache hashed static assets
steps: Create Cloudflare _headers file or Transform Rule
Pattern: /assets/*
Set: Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000, immutable
Verify with curl -I https://www.compresspic.top/assets/index-CyHHZpPs.js shows new header

items.title: Preload hero image and main JS bundle
steps: Add to <head> in scripts/prerender-locales.mjs:
<link rel='preload' as='image' href='/assets/frog-hero-bg-1600.webp' type='image/webp'>
<link rel='modulepreload' href='/assets/index-CyHHZpPs.js'>
Add srcset/sizes for responsive hero
Re-deploy, verify with Lighthouse

items.title: Expand CJK meta descriptions
steps: Edit src/locales/zh-CN.ts, zh-TW.ts, ja-JP.ts
Expand descriptions to 80-120 chars
Include: supported formats, batch, free, browser support
Re-build and re-deploy

items.title: Inline visible body content in prerender
steps: Extend scripts/prerender-locales.mjs to inject <h1>, <h2>, FAQ Q&A into <body>
Use parallel non-hydrated block (hidden visually, replaced by React on mount)
Or use <noscript> fallback for no-JS users
Validate with curl https://www.compresspic.top/ shows H1 in body

items.title: Add security headers via Cloudflare Transform Rules
steps: Add Response Header Rules for *:
X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff
Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin
X-Frame-Options: DENY
Permissions-Policy: camera=(), microphone=(), geolocation=()
Verify HSTS is on (Cloudflare SSL/TLS > Edge Certificates > HSTS)

items.title: Expand FAQ to 6-10 Q&A pairs
steps: Identify common questions: supported formats, max file size, browser support, mobile, comparison vs other tools
Add to FAQPage mainEntity in scripts/prerender-locales.mjs
Translate to all 9 locales

items.title: Enrich SoftwareApplication schema
steps: Add author, datePublished, softwareVersion, featureList to SoftwareApplication
Add separate Organization schema block
Validate with Google Rich Results Test

items.title: Add About and Privacy Policy pages
steps: Create src/pages/about.{ts,tsx}, src/pages/privacy.{ts,tsx}
Pre-render for each locale (or fall back to React routes with metadata)
Add to sitemap
Link from footer (visible in prerender)

items.title: Add 3-5 use-case landing pages
steps: /compress-for-email, /compress-for-web, /compress-for-whatsapp, /png-vs-webp
Each pre-rendered with localized title, description, FAQ
Add to sitemap (9 locales x 4-5 pages = 36-45 new URLs)
Internal linking between pages

items.title: Allow retrieval-only AI bots (OAI-SearchBot, PerplexityBot, Claude-User)
steps: Update robots.txt: add Allow: / for these bots
Keep Disallow: / for training-only bots (GPTBot, ClaudeBot, Google-Extended, etc.)
Add same Content-Signal: search=yes,ai-train=no
Monitor AI citation traffic in analytics

items.title: Responsive hero image variants
steps: Generate 800w and 1200w WebP variants of hero
Use <picture> with media queries or srcset
Update images.md in dist/ for OG (use 1600w)

items.title: Code-split Antd per component
steps: Replace `import { ... } from 'antd'` with direct imports in src/
Add @ant-design/icons selective imports
Verify build size with npm run build
Target: < 300 KB gzipped main bundle

items.title: Set up Google Search Console monitoring
steps: Verify https://compresspic.top/ in GSC
Submit sitemap.xml (after fixing canonical/www)
Monitor Coverage, Enhancements, Core Web Vitals reports
Watch for duplicate canonical issues

items.title: Set up Bing Webmaster Tools
steps: Add <meta name='msvalidate.01' content='...'> from Bing
Submit sitemap
Bing is the primary source of pre-AI-overviews organic traffic besides Google

items.title: Monitor AI citations
steps: Test queries in ChatGPT, Perplexity, Claude: 'best free image compressor that doesn't upload', '图片压缩不损画质', etc.
Track referral traffic from chat.openai.com, perplexity.ai, claude.ai in GA4
Iterate on llms.txt content based on citation patterns

items.title: Quarterly Lighthouse audit
steps: Run PageSpeed Insights on /, /en-US/, /ja-JP/
Track LCP, INP, CLS trends
Compare against field data in CrUX (after sufficient traffic)
